html:
EmptyCooking longCooking shortDoneBurnt

“A 4 slot container that will receive ingredients for food.

Placing 4 items in the container will allow them to be cooked creating some kind of food depending on the items put in to begin with.”

-

For Warly's unique cooking recipes, see Portable Crock Pot.
For Crock Pot Dishes, see Dishes.

The Crock Pot is a Structure used to cook a variety of belly-filling dishes from basic Foods. It requires 3 Cut Stone, 6 Charcoal, 6 Twigs and a Science Machine to prototype.

While enhancing Food's value and efficiency; it can even make use of otherwise inedible items like Twigs.

Usage

A Crock Pot has 4 slots for ingredients, each must be filled with a piece of Food to start cooking. Most recipes take 10–20 seconds to cook (Exact times for each recipe are listed in Dishes). Completed Crock Pot dishes can be stacked to 40; none of them can be used in the Crock Pot again.

A cooking attempt's ingredients have to match a recipe. If an attempt fails to match any recipe at all, Wet Goop ends up as the product.

When a recipe is attempted (i.e. the pot starts cooking), the Food Value the ingredients fit into are considered; almost all recipes require some amount of Food from certain Food Value, but there are many recipes with specific Food requirements (like Froggle Bunwich requires a Frog Leg).

Each recipe has recipe priorities, so a result can be decided when a set of ingredients fits more than one valid recipe.

When spoiling ingredients are used, the completed dish's spoil percentage becomes half the average spoil percentage of the ingredients (e.g. if 1 monster meat and 3 berries, all 90% spoiled (10% fresh), are placed in the Crock Pot, the resulting meatball will be 45% spoiled (55% fresh)). Non-spoilable ingredients are ignored when calculating average spoil percentage.

Crock Pot dishes start to spoil (even quicker in rain) before being collected. Some existing recipes gain the ability to warm or cool the player and new recipes have been added which can lower or raise the player's temperature. The Crock Pot itself also generates heat when cooking, which may Overheat the player during Summer.

Tips

  • The light emitted by an active Crock Pot is enough to keep Charlie away.
  • Warly has his own Portable Crock Pot.
  • Some Crock Pot dishes give fewer Hunger / Health points than the sum of their components.
    • For instance, it's better to eat four Roasted Carrots (+12 / +50 ), versus using them to make Ratatouille (+3 / +25 ); however, most Crock Pot recipes restore 5 , which may be desirable.
  • If the player has enough resources to create a 5-6 fit Crock Pot setting, the player can position them in a quintet or hexagonal style and place an Ice Box or two in the center. This will allow both the Ice Box and crock pot to be open simultaneously, increasing cooking efficiency.
  • It's a good idea to cook any stale or spoiled food on fire before putting them in the Crock Pot, if the recipe allows cooked food to be used. Doing so raises the overall freshness of the ingredients, making the end result last longer.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Heirloom Elegant Creepy Cauldron skin was only obtainable during official Hallowed Nights events until 2020.
  • The Woven - Elegant Terracotta Cooking Pot skin was introduced during the official Year of the Carrat event in 2020.
  • The Woven - Elegant Tureen skin was introduced during the official The Gorge event in 2018.
  • The Proof of Purchase Confectionery Cooker skin is obtained by purchasing the limited edition Pig House Gingerbread Mold from the Klei Shop.
  • The Loyal Celesturn Crock Pot skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of Don't Starve Together on Twitch from Dec 18, 2025 to Jan 15, 2026.

“It makes me hungry just to look at it.”
–Wilson
“All food must be cleansed with fire.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang will cook good meal for friends, meal like home!”
–Wolfgang
“As empty as my soul.”
–Wendy
“TO REFINE MEATS AND VEGETABLES INTO MORE ROBUST FORMS”
–WX-78
“It looks derelict when it's not cooking something.”
–Wickerbottom
“It seems a waste to just leave it sitting there, empty.”
–Woodie
“Just the thought makes my mouth water.”
–Maxwell
“Nöthing in there.”
–Wigfrid
“Food goes in, other food comes out.”
–Webber
“Empty pot, empty heart.”
–Warly
“Nothing there”
–Wormwood
“I make a mean stew.”
–Winona
“Can souls simmer?”
–Wortox
“Wish there was food inside, flurp.”
–Wurt
“Sure wish there was food in it.”
–Walter
“I guess I'll just... find some seeds to eat or something.”
–Wanda
“This is going to take a while.”
–Wilson
“The fire still has quite a bit of work to do.”
–Willow
“This take long time.”
–Wolfgang
“Whatever's in there isn't dead yet.”
–Wendy
“MORE TIME IS REQUIRED”
–WX-78
“It's got a bit to go before it's ready.”
–Wickerbottom
“It'll be a bit longer, eh?”
–Woodie
“Wait for it...”
–Maxwell
“Might as well dö sömething whilst I wait.”
–Wigfrid
“It won't be done for a while.”
–Webber
“A masterpiece takes time.”
–Warly
“Waiting...”
–Wormwood
“Still got a bit of a wait.”
–Winona
“It will take quite some time.”
–Wortox
“This take foreverrr...”
–Wurt
“This might take a while. Wanna see this cool bug I found in the meantime?”
–Walter
“This is why I hate cooking. Cooking takes forever!”
–Wanda
“It's almost done!”
–Wilson
“The fire is doing its thing!”
–Willow
“Is almost cook!”
–Wolfgang
“It needs to boil.”
–Wendy
“IT IS ALMOST COMPLETE”
–WX-78
“Almost done!”
–Wickerbottom
“Oh boy! Here it comes!”
–Woodie
“Here it comes!”
–Maxwell
“Shöuldn't be löng nöw!”
–Wigfrid
“Almost ready!”
–Webber
“Nearly there...”
–Warly
“Done soon”
–Wormwood
“Almost!”
–Winona
“I'm shivering with anticipation!”
–Wortox
“Will be done quick!”
–Wurt
“Almost time to eat!”
–Walter
“It has to be almost done by now, right?”
–Wanda
“Mmmmm! It's ready to eat!”
–Wilson
“Fire makes everything better. Mmm!”
–Willow
“Is time to eat!”
–Wolfgang
“Finally, food.”
–Wendy
“THE COOKING PROCESS IS DONE”
–WX-78
“Supper time!”
–Wickerbottom
“Time for supper!”
–Woodie
“Finally, some quality grub.”
–Maxwell
“What have we here?”
–Wigfrid
“Supper is served.”
–Webber
“Ahh, fini!”
–Warly
“Done!”
–Wormwood
“Soup's on!”
–Winona
“Looks like my treat is ready to eat!”
–Wortox
“Time for yums!”
–Wurt
“Come and get it!”
–Walter
“At long last, food!”
–Wanda
“The pot got cooked.”
–Wilson
“At least it went out in a blaze of glory.”
–Willow
“Pot is dead.”
–Wolfgang
“Burnt to a crisp.”
–Wendy
“POT MALFUNCTIONING”
–WX-78
“Now it's truly derelict.”
–Wickerbottom
“Burned to a crisp.”
–Woodie
“A bit overdone for my tastes.”
–Maxwell
“The fire reigned supreme.”
–Wigfrid
“Someone must have left the fire going.”
–Webber
“Tragique.”
–Warly
“Oh”
–Wormwood
“You guys like gristle, right?”
–Winona
“I don't understand how to make mortal food.”
–Wortox
“Oops...”
–Wurt
“It's uh... extra done!”
–Walter
“Well that's a setback.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Nồi Hầm là một Công trình được sử dụng để nấu nhiều món ăn no bụng từ các Thực phẩm cơ bản. Nó cần 3 Đá Cắt, 6 Than, 6 Cành Cây và Máy Khoa Học để tạo nguyên mẫu.

While enhancing [[Food]]'s value and efficiency; it can even make use of otherwise inedible items like [[Twigs]]. {{clear}} == {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage == A Crock Pot has 4 slots for ingredients, each must be filled with a piece of Food to start cooking. Most recipes take 10–20 seconds to cook (Exact times for each recipe are listed in [[Dishes]]). Completed Crock Pot dishes can be stacked to 40; none of them can be used in the Crock Pot again. A cooking attempt's ingredients have to match a recipe. If an attempt fails to match any recipe at all, [[Wet Goop]] ends up as the product. When a recipe is attempted (i.e. the pot starts cooking), the [[Food Value]] the ingredients fit into are considered; almost all recipes require some amount of Food from certain Food Value, but there are many recipes with specific Food requirements (like [[Froggle Bunwich]] requires a [[Frog Leg]]). Each recipe has recipe priorities, so a result can be decided when a set of ingredients fits more than one valid recipe. When spoiling ingredients are used, the completed dish's spoil percentage becomes half the average spoil percentage of the ingredients (e.g. if 1 monster meat and 3 berries, all 90% spoiled (10% fresh), are placed in the Crock Pot, the resulting meatball will be 45% spoiled (55% fresh)). Non-spoilable ingredients are ignored when calculating average spoil percentage. Crock Pot dishes start to spoil (even quicker in [[rain]]) before being collected. Some existing recipes gain the ability to warm or cool the player and new recipes have been added which can lower or raise the player's temperature. The Crock Pot itself also generates heat when cooking, which may [[Overheating|Overheat]] the player during [[Summer]]. == {{pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * The light emitted by an active Crock Pot is enough to keep [[Charlie]] away. * [[Warly]] has his own [[Portable Crock Pot]]. * Some Crock Pot dishes give fewer [[Hunger]] / [[Health]] points than the sum of their components. ** For instance, it's better to eat four [[Roasted Carrot]]s (+12 {{Hp}} / +50 {{Hunger}}), versus using them to make [[Ratatouille]] (+3 {{Hp}} / +25 {{Hunger}}); however, most Crock Pot recipes restore 5 {{Sanity}}, which may be desirable. *If the player has enough resources to create a 5-6 fit Crock Pot setting, the player can position them in a quintet or hexagonal style and place an Ice Box or two in the center. This will allow both the Ice Box and crock pot to be open simultaneously, increasing cooking efficiency. * It's a good idea to cook any stale or spoiled food on fire before putting them in the Crock Pot, if the recipe allows cooked food to be used. Doing so raises the overall freshness of the ingredients, making the end result last longer. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Gardening and Food Skins|Belongings}} * The '''Heirloom Elegant Creepy Cauldron''' skin was only obtainable during official [[Hallowed Nights]] events until 2020. * The '''Woven - Elegant Terracotta Cooking Pot''' skin was introduced during the official [[Year of the Carrat]] event in 2020. * The '''Woven - Elegant Tureen''' skin was introduced during the official [[The Gorge]] event in 2018. * The '''Proof of Purchase Confectionery Cooker''' skin is obtained by purchasing the limited edition Pig House Gingerbread Mold from the [[Merchandise|Klei Shop]]. * The '''Loyal Celesturn Crock Pot''' skin was awarded for watching 3 hours of ''Don't Starve Together'' on [[Streaming Drops|Twitch]] from Dec 18, 2025 to Jan 15, 2026.
{{SkinInfo |image = Creepy Cauldron Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |rarity2 = Heirloom Elegant |collection = Costume Collection |name = Creepy Cauldron |description = Double, double, broil and gobble, campfire burns and crockpot bubbles! |ingame = Creepy Cauldron.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Terracotta Cooking Pot Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Year of the Carrat Collection |name = Terracotta Cooking Pot |description = The earthy colors add warmth to your decor, as the fire adds warmth to your food. |ingame = Terracotta Cooking Pot.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Tureen Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Victorian Collection |name = Tureen |description = This crockpot is shaped like an elegant Victorian tureen. |ingame = Tureen.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Cottage Crock Pot Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Cottage Collection |name = Cottage Crock Pot |description = This crockpot is a must-have for any cottage, as is the cottage. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Camping Crock Pot Icon.png |rarity = Elegant |collection = Survivor Collection |name = Camping Crock Pot |description = This cooking pot looks kind of beaten up, but the food it cooks still tastes delicious. |ingame = Camping Crock Pot.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Confectionery Cooker Icon.png |rarity = Proof of Purchase |name = Confectionery Cooker |description = A little sugar never spoiled supper. }} {{SkinInfo |image = Celesturn Crock Pot Icon.png |rarity = Loyal |collection = Orbital Collection |name = Celesturn Crock Pot |description = Set your profile icon to a Celesturn Crock Pot. }}
